question: Does adding falsification-outcome bonus to science_quality.py improve quality signal accuracy? (L-900 prescription)
expect: science_quality.py falsification lanes score +0.5 bonus; L-900 prescription wired structurally
method: Added falsification_outcome score (1.0 if actual field contains 'falsified'/'null result'/'overturned', or mode=falsification) with +10% bonus to total. Updated criteria_means and JSON report.
actual: CONFIRMED: science_quality.py now rewards falsification outcomes. 16/786 experiments (2%) have falsified outcomes. Mean quality 26%→27.7% (+1.7pp). L-900 prescription structurally wired. B14 also retested (CONFIRMED with revision: 3-node sufficiency confirmed externally, determinism revised 74%→50-67% by L-699).
diff: Expected +0.5 score for falsification lanes — implemented as +0.10 bonus (capped at 1.0). Actual falsification outcome rate only 2% (16/786) — much lower than mode=falsification rate (4/1079 lanes). Most falsification happens in 'actual' field, not lane mode. Modest quality improvement (+1.7pp) because few experiments have explicit FALSIFIED outcomes.
